board.name: Sprint 42: Backend Core & Architecture
board.description: Mandatory sprint for Q2 KPIs. Strict adherence to DoD (Definition of Done) and workflow gates is required. All PRs must pass 90% test coverage threshold.

columns.name: Icebox
columns.description: Ideas and epics pending management approval

tasks.name: BE-901: Migrate orchestrator to Temporal.io
tasks.description: Epic is too large. Need architecture committee approval before grooming. Requires rewriting state machines in Go.

tasks.name: BE-905: Rust rewrite investigation
tasks.description: Management requested an evaluation to see if rewriting the matching engine in Rust will save AWS costs. Low priority.

columns.name: Grooming & Analysis
columns.description: Tasks requiring technical design docs before dev

tasks.name: BE-882: Define SLA for Kafka consumer lag
tasks.description: We need to mathematically prove our consumer group scaling logic can handle 50k msgs/sec under peak load. Need capacity planning doc.

tasks.name: BE-884: DB schema for polymorphic associations
tasks.description: DBA team requested a sync to discuss the new PostgreSQL sharding strategy for the audit_logs table to prevent table bloat.

columns.name: Ready for Dev
columns.description: Approved and estimated. Ready to be picked up.

tasks.name: BE-870: Distributed lock using Redis Redlock
tasks.description: Implement go-redsync/redsync. Ensure the background CRON workers do not overlap their jobs across the K8s cluster nodes.

tasks.name: BE-871: Graceful shutdown timeout
tasks.description: Increase terminationGracePeriodSeconds to 60s. Ensure context.WithTimeout handles inflight HTTP requests properly when SIGTERM is received.

tasks.name: BE-855: Optimize GC pause times (pprof)
tasks.description: Current pprof heap profile shows high allocation rate in the WebSocket hub. Implement sync.Pool for byte buffers to reduce Garbage Collector pressure.

tasks.name: BE-856: gRPC bidirectional streaming
tasks.description: Replace the current REST polling mechanism. Define Protobuf schemas, generate Go stubs, and implement thread-safe stream writers.

tasks.name: BE-860: Fix data race in DB pool
tasks.description: go test -race failed in CI pipeline. sync.RWMutex unlock is happening before the channel read. Fix the locking logic in internal/db/pool.go.

tasks.name: BE-840: Upgrade to Go 1.22
tasks.description: Blocked by the DevOps team. They need to update the base Docker images (golang:1.22-alpine) in the private CI registry before we can bump go.mod.

tasks.name: BE-830: Kafka Dead Letter Queue (DLQ)
tasks.description: Using IBM/sarama. Implemented retry logic with exponential backoff. PR is open, waiting for approvals.

columns.name: Code Review (Tech Lead)
columns.description: Requires Tech Lead / Staff Engineer sign-off

tasks.name: BE-825: CGO memory leak fix
tasks.description: PR #1042. Complex memory management. Tech Lead must review for potential memory leaks when freeing C++ pointers from Go using C.free(unsafe.Pointer(ptr)).

columns.name: QA Testing
columns.description: Deployed to staging, waiting for QA team

tasks.name: BE-810: Implement circuit breaker pattern
tasks.description: Using sony/gobreaker for external API calls to the payment gateway. QA needs to simulate 500/503 errors to verify state transitions (Closed -> Open -> Half-Open).

tasks.name: BE-799: JWT EdDSA signature migration
tasks.description: Migrating from HS256 to EdDSA (Ed25519) for cross-service token validation. Security team is performing pentesting on the staging environment.

columns.name: Ready for Release
columns.description: Merged to main, waiting for deployment window

tasks.name: BE-750: Distributed tracing (OpenTelemetry)
tasks.description: OTel spans added to all gRPC unary interceptors and pgx queries. Ready to be deployed in the next release window (Tuesday 2:00 AM).

tasks.name: BE-745: Fix N+1 query in GraphQL resolver
tasks.description: Implemented dataloader (graph-gophers/dataloader). Postgres CPU usage dropped by 40%.

tasks.name: BE-746: Rate Limiter via Token Bucket
tasks.description: Middleware using golang.org/x/time/rate deployed to production. Redis backend is stable, no memory spikes observed.
